meta.name: Banana Bread Loaf (Quick Bread Baseline)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: A reliable, moist banana bread loaf designed for high household adoption: one-bowl mixing, flexible add-ins, and clear doneness cues. Supports commercial flour or fine fresh-milled flour (choose one). Fresh-milled flour may require a small hydration adjustment and benefits from sifting coarse bran for a softer crumb.

ingredients.label: Very ripe bananas, mashed
ingredients.notes: About 3 large bananas (1 1/2 cups mashed). The riper (spotted/black), the sweeter and more aromatic.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, melted and cooled slightly
ingredients.notes: About 6 tbsp. Can substitute neutral oil for softer crumb.

ingredients.label: Light brown sugar
ingredients.notes: About 3/4 cup packed. For less sweet bread, use 120 g.

ingredients.label: Granulated sugar (optional, for sweeter loaf)
ingredients.notes: Optional: brings total sweetness closer to bakery-style.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt or sour cream
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup. Adds tenderness and moisture.

ingredients.label: Commercial flour option (use this OR the fresh-milled option): all-purpose flour
Fresh-milled flour option (use this OR the commercial option): fine-milled, sifted
ingredients.notes: Sift coarse bran for a softer crumb. Fresh-milled can absorb more; batter may need 15–30 ml extra milk if thick.

ingredients.label: Baking powder (optional, slightly taller loaf)
ingredients.notes: About 1 tsp. Optional; gives extra lift.

ingredients.label: Milk (only if needed for fresh-milled flour adjustment)
ingredients.notes: Add 15 ml at a time if batter is very thick; up to ~45 ml total depending on flour.

steps.title: Prep oven and pan
steps.body: Heat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a 9x5 loaf pan with parchment (with overhang) and lightly grease.

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the commercial flour option OR the fresh-milled flour option (not both). For fresh-milled flour, fine-mill and sift out coarse bran for best tenderness.

steps.title: Mix wet ingredients (one-bowl baseline)
steps.body: In a large bowl, mash bananas. Whisk in melted butter, brown sugar (and optional granulated sugar), eggs, vanilla, and yogurt/sour cream until smooth.

steps.title: Add dry ingredients
steps.body: Sprinkle flour, baking soda, optional baking powder, salt, and optional spices over the wet mixture. Fold with a spatula just until no dry flour remains. Do not overmix.

steps.title: Adjust batter for fresh-milled flour (if needed)
steps.body: If using fresh-milled flour and batter is very thick (stiff, hard to fold), add milk 15 ml at a time until batter is thick but scoopable. Rest 5 minutes to hydrate, then fold once gently.

steps.title: Fold in optional add-ins
steps.body: Fold in nuts and/or chocolate chips if using.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Scrape batter into pan and smooth the top. Bake 55–70 minutes until a tester comes out clean or with a few moist crumbs. If top browns too fast, tent with foil after ~40 minutes.

steps.title: Cool for best slice
steps.body: Cool in pan 15 minutes, then lift out and cool on a rack at least 45 minutes before slicing. Quick breads slice cleaner after fully cooled.

notes: High household adoption logic: one-bowl method, uses overripe bananas, and freezes well for quick breakfasts.
Doneness cue: center should be set; wet batter on the tester means it needs more time. Overbaking dries the loaf—check early.
Fresh-milled flour: sift coarse bran, expect higher absorption, and consider a short batter rest for better texture.
Flavor boost: add 1 tsp espresso powder or 1 tbsp dark rum (optional) for deeper aroma (not included by default).

storage.refrigerator: Wrapped airtight: up to 6 days (rewarm slices for best texture).
storage.freezer: Freeze whole loaf or slices airtight up to 2 months. Thaw at room temp or rewarm gently.
